households.source: ONS, Families and households in the UK, 2025 bulletin

population.source: ONS, Population estimates for the UK, mid-2024

annual_demand_twh.description: UK electricity final consumption by end users (TWh/year), used for the per-MWh-delivered framing. Total demand including losses was 320.2 TWh in 2025; the lower final-consumption figure is used so the per-MWh framing is not overstated by network losses.
annual_demand_twh.source: DESNZ Energy Trends, March 2026 statistical release (2025 annual data, section 5 / table 5.2)

nurse_salary_gbp.description: NHS Band 5 nurse annual salary, top of band (Agenda for Change, England, 2026-27). Top of band is used so nurse-equivalent counts are conservative.
nurse_salary_gbp.source: NHS Employers, Agenda for Change pay scales 2026/27

social_home_gbp.description: Gross upfront cost of building one social-rent home in England, including land (£35.4bn for 90,000 homes — £393,333 each). A full-cost figure is used rather than the much lower grant-per-home, so home counts round down.
social_home_gbp.source: Cebr for Shelter and the National Housing Federation, "The economic impact of building social housing" (2024)

hinkley_point_c_gbp.description: EDF's latest published cost estimate for Hinkley Point C (3.26 GW), £35bn in 2015 prices (2025 annual results, February 2026). The engine converts to 2024 prices with its own CPIH deflator series so the comparison shares the price basis of the numerator.
hinkley_point_c_gbp.source: New Civil Engineer, reporting EDF's 2025 annual results (20 February 2026)

deflator.source: ONS, CPIH Index 00 All Items 2015=100, annual averages (series L522)

bill.source: DESNZ, Digest of UK Energy Statistics (DUKES) 1.3, Sales of electricity and gas by sector

factoids.sentence: A year of direct UK renewable subsidy pays the salaries of 327,000 NHS nurses.
factoids.display_html: A year of this pays the salaries of 327,000 NHS nurses
factoids.label: NHS nurses' annual salaries paid by one year of direct renewable subsidy
factoids.source_name: NHS Employers, Agenda for Change pay scales 2026/27

factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 559,000 social homes — land included — in today’s money.
factoids.display_html: The £220bn+ full cost would have built 559,000 social homes, land included — in today’s money
factoids.label: social homes (land included) the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: Cebr for Shelter and the National Housing Federation, "The economic impact of building social housing" (2024)

factoids.sentence: The £220bn+ full cost of subsidising UK renewables, including estimated indirect costs, would have built 4 Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations in the UK, in today’s money.
factoids.display_html: — or 4 Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations
factoids.label: Hinkley Point C-scale nuclear stations the £220bn+ full UK renewables cost would have built, in today's money
factoids.source_name: New Civil Engineer, reporting EDF's 2025 annual results (20 February 2026)

factoids.sentence: Direct renewable subsidy adds £46.74 to every MWh of electricity delivered in the UK.
factoids.display_html: That is £46.74 on every MWh of electricity delivered
factoids.label: added to every MWh of electricity delivered in the UK by direct renewable subsidy
factoids.source_name: DESNZ Energy Trends, March 2026 statistical release (2025 annual data, section 5 / table 5.2)

factoids.sentence: Direct renewable subsidy costs every UK person £184.67 a year.
factoids.display_html: Per person, it is £184.67 a year (UK population)
factoids.label: per person per year — the direct cost of renewable subsidy to every UK resident
factoids.source_name: ONS, Population estimates for the UK, mid-2024
